name: Wayne Larsen
subject: HB 815 + the new 1802 — quick pre-season conversation?
body:
Hi Wayne,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1 — four offices up and down the Treasure Coast gives Tropical Roofing a footprint not many shops can match, and that's especially relevant heading into this season.

The reason I'm writing: HB 815 (effective July 1) and the April-revised wind mitigation form change the roofer pipeline in ways the homeowner-facing media hasn't fully caught up to yet. Roof inspections become a statutorily-required step before any age-based non-renewal, and metal systems with current credits documented are clearing closing tables faster than the general market. There's a real conversation between what you do on the install side and what I see on the claims side.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come up to Fort Pierce or meet anywhere along the corridor.

Best,
Duncan

name: Jacin Carreiro
subject: Quick intro before hurricane season
body:
Hi Jacin,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting in Wellington — public adjusting practice working homeowner-side claims across PBC, Broward, and Martin. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

The reason: a Boynton-based roofer + GC like SeaBreeze is in the sweet spot for the changes coming July 1 from HB 815 and the April-revised wind mitigation form (1802). Both move the homeowner conversation — and the closing-table conversation — in ways that benefit shops doing the complete work, not just the install.

Would you be open to a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Not a vendor pitch — just two operators in adjacent parts of the same flow comparing notes on where the volume is moving this season.

Happy to come to your office.

Best,
Duncan

name: Stacey Loftus
subject: Pre-season intro — 35 years deserves a hello
body:
Hi Stacey,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

The reason: 35 years in Mangonia Park with 24/7 emergency capacity puts Ranger Roofing in a specific category — the shops who actually answer the phone when something goes wrong are the ones I end up referring homeowner-side claim clients to. With HB 815 (effective July 1) and the new 1802 wind mitigation form already changing how carriers price age-based renewals, there's an interesting opening to make sure the right shops are top-of-mind when claim payouts hit.

Would you have time for a quick coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? Happy to come to you.

Best,
Duncan

name: Louis Toledo
subject: Quick intro — commercial roofing + claims overlap
body:
Hi (for Louis or Raju),

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1 hurricane season.

T&S's RCASF membership and commercial-portfolio depth puts you in a different category from most South FL roofers — and commercial roof claims are where the carrier underpayment is most predictable. With HB 815 effective July 1 and the new wind mitigation form revisions, the inspection and credit documentation conversation is changing in ways commercial property owners are going to start asking about.

Would either of you have 20 minutes for a coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Miami.

Best,
Duncan

name: Michael Nemati
subject: Pre-season intro — Robinhood Adjusting
body:
Hi Michael,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

The 6:30 AM operating hours stood out — that's a shop that gets work done before the rest of the trade wakes up. With HB 815 (effective July 1) and the April-revised 1802 wind mitigation form changing how carriers handle age-based renewals, the roofers who can move quickly on inspections are about to get a real volume tailwind.

Would you have time for a quick coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Not a vendor pitch — just two operators in the same flow comparing notes. Happy to come up to Oakland Park.

Best,
Duncan

name: Doug Roe
subject: Pre-season intro — multi-office roofer + claims side
body:
Hi Doug,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

The multi-office footprint (Stuart, Lake Worth, Winter Haven) puts Code Red in a few of my regular service areas at once. With HB 815 (effective July 1) and the new 1802 wind mitigation form already changing how carriers price age-based renewals, the volume conversation between roofers and the claims side is shifting in ways worth discussing.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come to whichever office is most convenient.

Best,
Duncan

name: Walter Flores
subject: Quick intro — Robinhood Adjusting
body:
Hi Walter,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1 hurricane season.

Integrity's PSL coverage put you in my service area, and with HB 815 effective July 1 and the April-revised wind mitigation form, the roofer-claims overlap is going to matter more this season than usual. The shops who can document credits and respond quickly to inspection requests are the ones I'd want to refer claim clients to.

Would you have time for a coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? Happy to come to wherever is convenient.

Best,
Duncan

name: Shawn Boyd
subject: Quick intro — claims side meets restoration side
body:
Hi Shawn,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

Stanley Restoration's WPB-to-Stuart corridor is right in my service area, and water/fire/mold work is where the carrier underpayment on the invoice side is most predictable. With three new property carriers landing in Florida this month and approved-vendor panels on all three opening in the next 6-12 months, this is the window for restoration shops to lock in early positioning.

Would you have time for a coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? My side is the homeowner-claims piece — would be useful to compare notes on what you're seeing on the carrier-payment side.

Happy to come to your office.

Best,
Duncan

name: Kathleen
subject: Pre-season intro — Royal Palm Beach neighbors
body:
Hi Kathleen,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington — practically neighbors with Remediation 911. Wanted to reach out before June 1.

Being licensed GC plus mold/water means Remediation 911 handles the full job in-house, which is rare and exactly the kind of shop I end up referring claim clients to when the carrier finally pays out and the homeowner needs the repair done right. Three new property carriers just landed in Florida this month, and approved-vendor panels on all three open in the next 6-12 months — worth getting in front of.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come over.

Best,
Duncan

name: Joshua Winton
subject: Quick intro — Broward/PBC/Dade overlap
body:
Hi Josh,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1 hurricane season.

Discreet Restoration's tri-county Broward/PBC/Dade footprint overlaps almost exactly with where I work claims, and the fact that the owner is reachable on a cell is the kind of detail that matters when a claim payout hits and a homeowner needs the repair started this week, not next month. With three new carriers landing in Florida and vendor panels opening in the next 6-12 months, this is a useful window.

Would you have time for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Happy to come to Sunrise.

Best,
Duncan

name: Brandon Porter
subject: Pre-season intro — WPB SERVPRO + claims side
body:
Hi Brandon,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

Locally-owned franchises (vs. corporate referrals from the national line) are the ones I tend to recommend when a homeowner needs immediate water mitigation — and SERVPRO of West Palm Beach has the local-owner reputation. With three new carriers landing in Florida this month, approved-vendor panels are about to start opening, and the timing is good for restoration firms to be on top of the list.

Would you have time for a quick coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? Happy to come over.

Best,
Duncan

name: Phil Viruso
subject: Pre-season intro — succession stories deserve a hello
body:
Hi Phil,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

Taking over from Ron Lowen after 49 years is the kind of transition I have a lot of respect for — the shops that survive a generational handoff and keep the reputation intact are the ones I want in my referral network. With hurricane season starting in five days and a lot of homeowners about to have AC + electrical claims after the first named storm, this is a good window to compare notes.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come to WPB.

Best,
Duncan

name: Robert Bradley
subject: Pre-season intro — 45 years deserves a hello
body:
Hi Robert (and Kristin),

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

Family-owned since 1980 with a husband-wife president/VP team puts Bradley HVAC in the category of shops I actively try to keep in front of when AC + electrical claims come up after a named storm. With three new property carriers landing in Florida this month, approved-vendor panels are about to start opening for HVAC trades, and this is the window to be top-of-mind.

Would you have time for a coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come to RPB.

Best,
Duncan

name: Victor Hugo Finalet
subject: Pre-season intro — Miami-Dade + Broward HVAC
body:
Hi Victor,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

Saturday hours plus Miami-Dade + Broward coverage puts Freezing Mechanical in the category of HVAC shops I'd want to refer claim clients to — most homeowners need same-week service after a storm, and most shops aren't set up for that. With three new property carriers landing in Florida this month and approved-vendor panels opening, this is the right window.

Would you have time for a quick coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Miami Gardens.

Best,
Duncan

name: Robert Beans
subject: Quick intro — Coral Gables Chamber neighbor
body:
Hi Robert,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice based in Wellington. Reaching out ahead of June 1.

A Coral Gables Chamber trustee running a family-owned plumber is the kind of shop I want in my referral network — water leak claims are some of the most predictable post-storm work, and the homeowner usually needs a plumber on-site within 48 hours. With three new property carriers landing in Florida and approved-vendor panels opening, this is a good window for an introduction.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Coral Gables.

Best,
Duncan

name: Emilio Furones
subject: Pre-season intro — Robinhood Adjusting
body:
Hi Emilio,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

The detail that the owner still answers the phones at All Florida Plumbing stood out — that's rare these days, and it's exactly the kind of shop I want to know about for hydro-jetting and drain work after a water claim. Three new property carriers just landed in Florida and approved-vendor panels open in the next 6-12 months.

Would you have time for a quick coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Miami.

Best,
Duncan

name: Brian McEntee
subject: Quick intro — Wellington-area overlap
body:
Hi Brian,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

Associated Property Management covering 130+ associations from Jupiter to Boca puts Wellington squarely in your footprint, and association managers tend to be first to hear about property damage across a portfolio. With hurricane season starting in five days, this is the window where I'd love to be useful when a damage situation comes up at one of your properties — second-opinion-on-the-claims-payout is the typical entry point.

Would you have time for a coffee or 15-minute call in the next couple of weeks? Happy to come to Lake Worth.

Best,
Duncan

name: Jason Schoenholtz
subject: Quick intro — HOA mgmt + brokerage + claims
body:
Hi Jason,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

Running both Trident Management (HOA/condo) and Trident Real Estate puts you in a rare position — most operators in your space see either the property side or the transaction side, not both. With hurricane season starting in five days, second-opinion-on-claim-payouts is the typical entry point with managers I work with on the HOA side, and a similar story shows up at the closing table on the brokerage side.

Would you have time for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Miami Beach.

Best,
Duncan

name: Chris Kennedy
subject: Quick intro — Fort Lauderdale residential PM
body:
Hi Chris (and Karin),

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

Residential property managers tend to have the earliest visibility into damage across a portfolio — usually before the owner has decided whether to file a claim. With hurricane season starting in five days, this is the window where I'd love to be useful: second-opinion-on-claim-payouts is the typical entry point, no charge for a first look.

Would you have time for a coffee or quick call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come down to Fort Lauderdale.

Best,
Duncan

name: Jean Adolphe
subject: Quick intro — Boynton-area HOA management
body:
Hi Jean,

I'm Duncan with Robinhood Adjusting, public adjusting practice in Wellington. Reaching out before June 1.

JMA's PBC + Broward HOA/POA/condo footprint overlaps almost exactly with where I work claims, and association managers tend to be first to hear about property damage at the unit and common-element level. With hurricane season starting in five days, this is the window where I'd love to be useful to your associations when claim payouts get challenged.

Would you have 20 minutes for a coffee or call in the next couple of weeks? Easy for me to come to Boynton.

Best,
Duncan
